general_feedback: The filled-in @return descriptions are accurate, concise, and consistent with the surrounding documentation style. Each one correctly reflects what the code returns: `apply` describes the fallback to `defaultEntry(key)`; `default` notes the throwing behavior when no `defaultEntry` was supplied; `addOne` and `transformValuesInPlace` correctly describe returning `this`; the various `mapValuesNow`/`map`/`flatMap`/`collect` returns describe new `AnyRefMap` instances; and the companion-object factories (`apply`, `empty`, `withDefault`, `fromZip`) clearly describe the constructed collection, including the shorter-array/collection truncation semantics in `fromZip`. Style is consistent: lowercase starts, backticks for code references, and one-sentence form throughout.
